title: Duraluxe Metal Panels | Changes to Metal Sublimation
description:
If your metal sublimation panels are chipping at the edges, cracking under flex, or falling apart after cutting, you're using the wrong panels. Duraluxe uses a powder-coated surface that stays intact through cutting, flexing, and outdoor exposure, backed by a five-year UV color fading warranty for exterior applications. No VOCs, Italian manufacturing, and US production coming in 2027.

Beyond durability, Duraluxe offers nine distinct finishes including gloss, matte, satin, textured, Ice Touch, Wall Sky, Salt Lake, glow in the dark, and brush matte. These finishes open up applications like retail displays, interior decor, hospitality projects, and architectural signage that standard metal prints simply can't touch.

In this video we cover everything you need to get the best results. Heat press calibration, why Nomex outperforms parchment paper, why 70 to 80 GSM transfer paper beats heavier multi-use papers, and why pressing face down is one of the most important changes you can make for consistent output.
